questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A solid sphere of radius $=1 \mathrm{~m}$ has heat source throughout the volume such that heat is generated uniformly per unit volume inside it at a constant rate of $1.5 \mathrm{KW} / \mathrm{m}^3$. At steady state the outer surface temperature is at $30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Find the maximum temperature $\left({ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$ in the sphere at steady state. Thermal conductivity of this sphere $=10 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{m}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Assume that heat flows uniformly in radial direction.
questions.solution:
Consider a concentric smaller sphere of radius $r$ within the given sphere Heat generated $w \times \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3$ where $w$ is heat generation rate per unit volume.
$\mathrm{w} \times \frac{4}{3} \pi \mathrm{r}^3=\mathrm{K} 4 \pi \mathrm{r}^2\left(-\frac{\mathrm{dT}}{\mathrm{dr}}\right)$
$\mathrm{dT}=-\frac{\mathrm{w}}{3 \mathrm{~K}} \mathrm{rdr}$
$\therefore T=-\frac{w}{6 K} r^2+C$
at $r=R$ $\qquad \mathrm{T}=\mathrm{T}_0 \quad \Rightarrow \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{T}_0+\frac{\mathrm{w}}{6 \mathrm{~K}} \mathrm{R}^2$
$\mathrm{T}=\mathrm{T}_0+\frac{\mathrm{W}}{6 \mathrm{~K}}\left(\mathrm{R}^2-\mathrm{r}^2\right)$
$\mathrm{T}=\mathrm{T}_{\max }$ when $\mathrm{r}=0$ (at the center)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small sphere $\left(\right.$ emissivity $=0.9$, radius $\left.=r_1\right)$ is located at the centre of a spherical asbestos shell (thickness $=5.0 \mathrm{~cm}$, outer radius $=\mathrm{r}_2$ ). The thickness of the shell is small compared to the inner and outer radii of the shell. The temperature of the small sphere is 800 K , while the temperature of the inner surface of the shell is 600 K . The temperature of small sphere is maintained constant. Assuming that $\frac{r_2}{r_1}=10.0$ and ignoring any air inside the shell, find the temperature (in K ) of the outer surface of the shell. Take : $\mathrm{K}_{\text {asbestos }}=0.085 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{m}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, \sigma=\frac{17}{3} \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{m}^2 \mathrm{k}^4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two containers A and B are connected by a conducting solid cylindrical rod of length $\frac{242}{7} \mathrm{~cm}$ and radius $\sqrt{8.3} \mathrm{~cm}$. Thermal conductivity of the rod is 693 watt/mole-K. The container A contains two mole of oxygen gas and the container B contains four mole of helium gas. At time $t=0$ temperature difference of the containers is $50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, after what time (in seconds) temperature difference between them will be $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Transfer of heat takes place through the rod only. Neglect radiation loss. Take $\mathrm{R}=8.3 \mathrm{~J} /$ mole -K and $\pi=\frac{22}{7}$.
questions.solution:
Thermal resistance of the rod,
$\mathrm{R}=\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{kA}}$
When heat is transferred from first vessel to second, temperature of first vessel decreases while that of second vessel increases. Due to both there reasons, difference between temperature of vessels decreases.
Let at an instant t , the temperature difference between two vessels be $\theta$.
$H=\frac{\theta}{R}=\frac{K A \theta}{\ell}$
$\mathrm{dQ}=\mathrm{Hdt}=\frac{\mathrm{KA} \theta}{\ell} \mathrm{dt}$    ....(i)
Since gases are contained in two vessels, therefore, processes on gases in two vessels are isochoric.
Hence, decrease in temperature of gas in first vessel,
$\Delta \theta_1=\frac{d Q}{n C_V}=\frac{d Q}{2 \times \frac{5 R}{2}}=\frac{d Q}{5 R}$
Increase in temperature of gas in second vessel is
$\Delta \theta_2=\frac{d Q}{4 \times \frac{3 R}{2}}=\frac{d Q}{6 R}$
$\therefore \quad$ Decrease in temperature difference $(-\mathrm{d} \theta)=\Delta \theta_1+\Delta \theta_2$
$-d \theta=\frac{d Q}{R} \times \frac{11}{30}$
or, $-\mathrm{d} \theta=\frac{\mathrm{KA} \theta \times 11}{30 \mathrm{R} \ell} \mathrm{dt}$
or $\quad-\int_{50}^{25} \frac{\mathrm{d} \theta}{\theta}=\frac{\mathrm{KA} \times 11^{\mathrm{t}}}{30 \ell \mathrm{R}} \int_0^{\mathrm{d}} \mathrm{dt}$
or, $\ell \mathrm{n} 2=\frac{\mathrm{KA} \times 11}{30 \ell \mathrm{R}} \mathrm{t}$
or $t=\frac{.693 \times 30 \times 242 \times 10^{-2} \times 8.3 \times 7}{693 \times 7 \times 22 \times 8.3 \times 10^{-4} \times 11}=3$ seconds.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A rod of negligible heat capacity has length 40 cm , area of cross-section $1.0 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ and thermal conductivity $100 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{m}^{\circ}{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The temperature of one end is maintained at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and that of the other end is slowly and linearly varied from $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $60^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ in 10 minutes. Assuming no loss of heat through the sides, find the total heat transmitted through the rod in these 10 minutes (in J).
questions.solution:
Temperature as a function of time $\mathrm{T}=\frac{1}{10} t^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
$\frac{d Q}{d t}=k A \frac{d T}{d x} \Rightarrow \int_0^Q d Q=\int_0^{(10 \times 60)}\left(\frac{k A}{0.4}\right) 0.1 t d t=\left.\frac{100 \times 1 \times 10^{-4} \times 0.1 t^2}{0.8}\right|_0 ^{600}=450$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A metal block is placed in a room which is at $10^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ for long time. Now it is heated by an electric heater of power 500 W till its temperature becomes $50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Its initial rate of rise of temperature is $2.5^{\circ} \mathrm{C} /$ sec . The heater is switched off and now a heater of 100 W is required to maintain the temperature of the block at $50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The heat radiated per second when the block was $30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is given as $\alpha$ watt. Find the value of $\left(\frac{\alpha}{10}\right)$. (Assume Newtons Law of cooling to be valid)
questions.solution:
Given at $50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ : Newton's Law of cooling
Power Loss $=100=k(50-10) \Rightarrow k=\frac{10}{4}=\frac{5}{2}$
$\therefore \text { At } 30^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \mathrm{P}_{\text {Loss }}=\mathrm{k}(30-10)=\frac{5}{2} \times 20=50 \mathrm{~W}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Two conductors $A$ and $B$ each of cross section area $5 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ are connected in series. Variation of temperature (in ${ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ ) along the length (in cm ) is as shown in the figure. If thermal conductivity of $A$ is 200 $\mathrm{J} / \mathrm{m}$-sec- ${ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and the heat current in the conductors is $0.173 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{s}$. The thermal conductivity of $B$ is $K \times 50 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{m}-\mathrm{sec}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Find the value of K.
